styleHead: STYLE LOCK: Period gothic live-action, mid-19th-century interiors; candlelight and deep shadows; desaturated cool-gray palette; naturalistic skin and fabric texture; no illustration or stylized animation
castCountLine: CAST COUNT: exactly 1 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Narrator. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.
actionText: INT. OLD MAN'S BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. A long stretch. THE NARRATOR listens, head tilted. The muffled beat. slows. falters. Silence. He heaves the bed aside. Kneels. Places his hand upon the old man's heart. Holds it there. Many minutes. No pulsation. OFF-CAMERA VOICEOVER Character_The_Narrator says "There was no pulsation. He was stone dead. His eye would trouble me no more.". Character_The_Narrator still wears loose dark nightshirt, barefoot, holding lantern Camera directive: Close-up on Narrator’s relieved face: no pulsation, stone dead, the eye will trouble him no more. Performance: Acting intensity 4/10: Deep relief softens face, brief peaceful eye-close, gentle true smile Optics: f/1.4 shallow depth of field, finality bokeh Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219, crushed charcoal shadows, cold blue-black tones, single sickly amber lantern ray
prompt:
STYLE LOCK: Period gothic live-action, mid-19th-century interiors; candlelight and deep shadows; desaturated cool-gray palette; naturalistic skin and fabric texture; no illustration or stylized animation

CHARACTER VARIABLES (use these identities consistently; do not redesign faces or wardrobe):
- Character_The_Narrator [The Narrator]: Lean man of middle years (about 40–50), pale sallow skin, hollow cheeks, dark disordered medium-length hair, bright intense dark eyes, thin tense mouth; plain dark wool waistcoat over white shirtsleeves, dark trousers; period clothing. Visual lock: Same lean pale face, dark disordered hair, and bright intense dark eyes in every scene; always plain dark waistcoat and shirtsleeves as default; never elderly, never white-haired, never the filmed blue eye. Voice: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O.

CAST COUNT: exactly 1 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Narrator. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.

AUDIO: REQUIRED native Grok off-camera voiceover. Character_The_Narrator narrates exactly: "There was no pulsation. He was stone dead. His eye would trouble me no more.". Start speaking immediately with "There" — do not skip, delay, or swallow the opening word. After the last word, hold a brief natural pause with a closed mouth (about half a second); do not freeze mid-syllable or trail into empty staring. Do not lip-sync on-screen cast to this VO. Ambient bed: muffled heartbeat. VOICE LOCK Character_The_Narrator: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O.

PREVIOUS CLIP (already provided as video input — continue from its last frame):
INT. OLD MAN'S BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT. A long stretch. THE NARRATOR listens, head tilted. The muffled beat. slows. falters. Silence. He heaves the bed aside. Kneels. Places his hand upon the old man's heart. Holds it there. Many minutes. No pulsation. OFF-CAMERA VOICEOVER Character_The_Narrator says "I placed my hand upon the heart and held it there many minutes.". Character_The_Narrator still wears loose dark nightshirt, barefoot, holding lantern Camera directive: Close-up of Narrator’s hand pressed firmly on the old man’s heart, holding for many silent minutes.
